attacking_style: Primarily a bunch sprinter who can finish explosively in final meters, not a traditional attacker. Capable of taking stage wins from reduced sprints rather than solo moves.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with limited team support given contract-hunting status. Relies on individual positioning and sprint timing rather than lead-out trains. Uses GC positioning opportunistically without dedicated team protection.
terrain_preference: Excels in bunch sprint finishes and wet/technical conditions. Can handle short stage races with moderate GC demands. Struggles to convert sprint speed into outright victories against top-tier sprinters.
vulnerability: Lacks the ultimate top-end speed to consistently beat elite sprinters like Maricka—repeatedly finishes 2nd-3rd. GC ambitions appear secondary when stage opportunities arise, suggesting limited climbing strength for mountain stages.
key_patterns: Consistently places 2nd-3rd in sprints across multiple stage races (Tour de Suisse, Tour de Pologne)
Performs under contract pressure, converting desperation into wins in 2017 (Romandie, Tour de Suisse Stage 1)
Maintains GC positions within 20 seconds without prioritizing overall classification over stage hunting
Wins from reduced bunch sprints rather than mass field gallops, suggesting positioning over pure speed
Can perform post-injury or below peak condition, showing resilience and opportunism
form_trajectory: neutral: Mid-2017 shows consistent placement ability but stagnation at runner-up positions. Early wins demonstrate capability but inability to repeat against top sprinters suggests plateau rather than progression.
